title: Geolocation
description: Check your understanding of how a user's geolocation can be tracked in this set of free practice questions designed for AP Computer Science Principles students.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mariana gives a social media website access to her geolocation so that she can quickly see local events happening around her.

**Which is NOT a risk of granting geolocation access?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The website could begin tracking Mariana's browsing history as well.
The website could share her geolocation with her friends.
The website could post the geolocation on her public profile.
Employees of the social media company could see the geolocation of Mariana.
If a hacker gets access to the company's database, they could see Mariana's geolocation.

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2016, the ACLU reported that a company called Geofeedia analyzed data from social media websites like Twitter and Instagram to find users involved in local protests and sold that data to police forces. The data included usernames and their approximate geographic locations. Geofeedia claims the data was all publicly available data.

In response to the news, many users were eager to find ways to keep their geolocation private.

**Which of these actions is _least_ likely to help a user keep their geolocation private?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Stripping metadata from their photos before uploading them
Refusing to grant access to websites asking for their geolocation
Removing location information from their profile
Using a VPN (Virtual Private Network) to mask their IP while browsing the web
Disabling third-party cookies in their browser

assessment_items.question_content:
Billy receives a message from a stranger on a social media website that simply says "I know where you live." Billy tried to think about his online activities, and whether or not those activities could have revealed his location.

**Which of these online activities is *least* likely to have revealed his location to the stranger?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Reading news sites in a browser with third-party cookies enabled.
Granting the social media site access to their geolocation while searching for nearby posts.
Uploading a photo of their house, as taken from the street.
Uploading a photo of their cat sleeping in the garden (with no house visible).
Looking up driving directions to their house on a mapping site over an unsecured HTTP connection.

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2018, more than a thousand data breaches were reported and over 500 million data records were exposed in those breaches. 

In one particular breach, a video making website discovered unauthorized access to their database. The attackers were able to access the following user details: first name, last name, username, geolocation, gender, and date of birth.

**Which of the following is an immediate risk of that data breach?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 2]]

options.explanation:
“2018 Year End Data Breach Report.” Identity Theft Resource Center. CyberScout. Accessed May 12, 2020. https://www.idtheftcenter.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/02/ITRC_2018-End-of-Year-Aftermath_FINAL_V2_combinedWEB.pdf.

Whittaker, Zack. “Animoto Hack Exposes Personal Information, Location Data.” TechCrunch. TechCrunch, August 20, 2018. https://techcrunch.com/2018/08/20/animoto-hack-exposes-personal-information-geolocation-data/.

choices.content: A stalker could go to the house of one of the website users.
A cybercriminal could take control of the access point of one of the website users, turning it into a rogue access point.
A hacker could install keylogging software on the machine of one of the website users.
A cybercriminal could use the credit card information of one of the website users to make an online purchase.

assessment_items.question_content:
The recent rise in popularity of smart home products (such as smart home assistants or smart thermostats) has led to an increase in concerns about their security.

In June 2019, security researchers discovered an openly accessible database from a smart home products company. Anyone could potentially access that database, without a username or password, and see all the data.

The database records included these details:

* Email addresses
* Passwords
* Username
* Precise geolocation
* IP address
* Family name
* Family ID
* Smart device name

**Which of the following are immediate risks of malicious access to that database?**

👁️**Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: “Report: Orvibo Smart Home Devices Leak Billions of User Records.” vpnMentor. Accessed April 8, 2020. https://www.vpnmentor.com/blog/report-orvibo-leak/.

choices.content: A cybercriminal could log into a user's account on the smart home product website and change the behavior of one of their smart devices.
A burglar could break into a user's home and steal the smart home devices.
A hacker could install keylogging software onto a user's laptop.
A cybercriminal could log into a user's bank account, even those accounts that require multi-factor authentication.

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2019, a security research firm discovered that it was possible to monitor the geographic location of users in four dating apps. All they needed to know was their username and they could map the most recent location of the user.

**Which of the following risk becomes possible due to the accessibility of the geolocation data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Seals, Tara. “4 Dating Apps Pinpoint Users' Precise Locations – and Leak the Data.” Threatpost English Global threatpostcom. Accessed April 8, 2020. https://threatpost.com/4-dating-apps-pinpoint-precise-locations/147247/.

choices.content: A stalker could find a user that rejected them and follow them around.
A cybercriminal could steal the identity of a user and open a bank account in their name.
A hacker could log into the account of a nearby user and trick other users into dates with them.
An attacker could retrieve the user's previous locations from the databases of the dating apps.

assessment_items.question_content:
A company gives phones for business use to all of its salespeople. The IT department installs a suite of security software on each phone that includes a monitoring program that sends the phone's current location back to company headquarters.

**Assuming the employees always have their phone near them, which of these questions could _not_ be answered by the location data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Where is the employee currently located?
What path did an employee take from home to work?
How long did an employee spend in the restaurant during lunch time?
How many customers did the employee go to lunch with?

assessment_items.question_content:
A company develops a mobile app designed for parents to monitor what their children are doing. The parents install the app on the phone, and it can record details like who the child is conversing with and what apps they're using. It can also record and store the geolocation of the child.

**Which of these questions from parents could _not_ be answered by the recorded geolocation data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Did my child break their curfew by staying outside of the home past 9pm?
Did my child travel to another city today?
Did my child spend more than an hour outside of the school campus in the middle of the day?
Did my child hang out in the park in a group of more than 20 people?

assessment_items.question_content:
Apple, the producer of iPhones, includes a feature called Location Services that can be used by apps.

The following snippet is from their article on the feature:

>Location Services allows Apple and third-party apps and websites to gather and use information based on the current location of your iPhone or Apple Watch to provide a variety of location-based services. For example, an app might use your location data and location search query to help you find nearby coffee shops or theaters, or your device may set its time zone automatically based on your current location.

>To use features such as these, you must enable Location Services on your iPhone and give your permission to each app or website before it can use your location data. Apps may request limited access to your location data (only when you are using the app) or full access (even when you are not using the app).

A user installs an app that requests full access to Location Services and grants the requested permissions.

**What location-related data can be stored by the app?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The app can access the user's current location, but they cannot store that data.
The app can only store the user's most recent location.
The app can store all the locations visited by the user while they have the app open, but not the locations visited while the app is closed.
The app can store all the locations visited by the user while they have their phone with them.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kadijah is concerned about her geolocation getting tracked and stored in a database.

**To avoid her geolocation being tracked, which computing devices should she avoid using?**

* $\text{I}$. Devices on a wired Internet connection
* $\text{II}$. Devices on a wireless Internet connection
* $\text{III}$. Devices with a GPS receiver

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: $\text{I}$ only
$\text{I}$ and $\text{III}$ only
$\text{II}$ and $\text{III}$ only
$\text{I}$, $\text{II}$, and $\text{III}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Kimberly is trying to understand which of her devices and apps can collect information about her location.

**Which of the following statements is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: If a company's privacy policy does not mention location data, then their app cannot collect information about Kimberly's location.
If a device has no connection to the Internet, then it cannot collect information about Kimberly's location.
If a device has no GPS receiver, then it cannot collect information about Kimberly's location.

assessment_items.question_content:
A privacy-concerned citizen wants to introduce local regulations to restrict companies that collect the geolocation data of their users. They would like every company to present users with a warning and explanation before collecting the data.

**Which table identifies the types of companies that would be affected by those regulations?**

_Each table lists four types of companies in the same order._

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content:
Who | Affected?
- | - | -
Website developers | Yes
Smartphone manufacturers | Yes
Mobile app developers | Yes
Internet Service Providers | Yes

Who | Affected?
- | - | -
Website developers | No
Smartphone manufacturers | Yes
Mobile app developers | Yes
Internet Service Providers | Yes

Who | Affected?
- | - | -
Website developers | Yes
Smartphone manufacturers | Yes
Mobile app developers | Yes
Internet Service Providers | No

Who | Affected?
- | - | -
Website developers | No
Smartphone manufacturers | Yes
Mobile app developers | No
Internet Service Providers | Yes
